title: pastinaca zozimoides
intro: Pastinaca zozimoides, also known as Za'atar or Wild Cumin, is a plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal and culinary properties, particularly in Middle Eastern and Mediterranean cultures. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and seeds are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as added to salads and dishes for their flavor and aroma.
